concerns.text: FR-006 requires re-running the 'entire matching and aggregation process' for sensitivity analysis. T044 describes re-matching and re-aggregating but omits the re-execution of the ingestion pipeline (T023/T015) and fallback logic (FR-008). If a new Levenshtein threshold alters the dataset composition significantly, the fallback check must be re-evaluated to prevent false triggers or empty datasets, as mandated by EC-001.

FR-007 mandates a 'block-permutation' test shuffling scores 'among tracks' while preserving User-Track grouping. T045 instructs shuffling scores 'among User-Track pairs', which breaks the block structure and invalidates the null distribution logic. Additionally, T045 outputs a pre-calculated p-value per iteration, whereas FR-007 requires establishing a full null distribution to compare the observed statistic against.

EC-002 requires filtering 'Tracks with high exposure but zero memory cues'. T027 describes filtering 'tracks with high exposure but no memory cues' without explicitly linking this to the 'User-Track Pair' level aggregation (FR-004) or defining the 'high exposure' threshold. This risks applying the filter at the wrong granularity (track-level vs pair-level) or with undefined criteria.

Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) requires external citations to be verified by the Reference-Validator Agent. T012a attempts to verify AMT data integrity solely by checking a local metadata field `source_type == "human"`, bypassing the mandated agent-based citation verification for the dataset source itself.

Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) requires random seeds to be pinned. T045 specifies running 1000 iterations for the permutation test but does not explicitly mandate pinning the random seed for this process, making the null distribution non-reproducible.
